findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 432 of 432 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 1774 of 1774 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'leading_or_trailing_whitespaces' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Leading or trailing whitespaces (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_same_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance same coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_route' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Same name and description for route (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Two fields, likely set once in your export settings.
findings.fix: Fill in feed_start_date and feed_end_date in feed_info.txt.
findings.what: feed_info.txt does not state when this feed starts and ends.
findings.why: Without stated dates, apps can't warn anyone before your data goes stale; it just disappears one day.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_extends_far_in_the_future' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Service extends far in the future (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unknown_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unknown file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 8 kinds of issue across 519 instances (0 error, 513 warning, 6 informational).

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 26851 days.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 26851 days.
States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 26851 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 8 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (RD, OG, GD, AQ, TN, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 385 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("Vernon & Sugar Creek", "Fort Jesse & Susan", "Greenbriar & Jacobssen", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.
